description: Data loaded from CSV file: 97.csv (Dataset: Supply Chain Analysis)

fields.name: Product type
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field categorizes each product in the supply chain by its general category within the beauty and personal care industry, such as cosmetics, haircare, or skincare.

fields.name: SKU
fields.sample_values: SKU0, SKU1, SKU10, SKU11, SKU12, ..., SKU95, SKU96, SKU97, SKU98, SKU99
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: SKU is a unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each distinct product or item tracked within the supply chain for inventory management and analysis.

fields.name: Price
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the monetary cost or selling price of individual supply chain items or goods, likely measured in units of currency per item or batch.

fields.name: Availability
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Availability" field likely represents the percentage level (1–100%) of product, material, or resource stock that is currently accessible or on hand within the supply chain.

fields.name: Number of products sold
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the total quantity of individual products sold per transaction or reporting period within a supply chain, indicating sales volume for inventory or distribution analysis.

fields.name: Revenue generated
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the total monetary income, in some currency, earned from supply chain activities or transactions, likely per client, order, or time period within the supply chain analysis context.

fields.name: Customer demographics
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the gender identity of customers within the supply chain, categorizing them as Female, Male, Non-binary, or Unknown for demographic analysis.

fields.name: Stock levels
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Stock levels represent the current quantity of inventory units available for a product or item at a specific location within the supply chain, ranging from out-of-stock (0) to fully stocked (100) units.

fields.name: Lead times
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the number of days required from placing an order to receiving goods or materials in the supply chain process.

fields.name: Order quantities
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: The "Order quantities" field represents the number of units of goods requested per individual order in a supply chain transaction, typically indicating the volume of items purchased by customers or clients in each order.

fields.name: Shipping times
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the duration, likely in days, taken for goods to be shipped within a supply chain process, providing a measure of delivery speed for each transaction or order.

fields.name: Shipping carriers
fields.sample_values: Carrier A, Carrier B, Carrier C
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the logistics companies responsible for transporting goods within the supply chain, distinguishing shipments by their carrier (e.g., Carrier A, Carrier B, Carrier C).

fields.name: Shipping costs
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the monetary expense incurred per shipment in the supply chain, likely measured in consistent currency units, reflecting the cost to transport goods between locations.

fields.name: Supplier name
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies the specific suppliers involved in the supply chain, serving as categorical labels for entities providing goods or services to the business.

fields.name: Location
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the major Indian cities where supply chain operations, such as warehouses or distribution centers, are located or managed.

fields.name: Lead time
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: Lead time represents the number of days required to fulfill an order or restock inventory in the supply chain, reflecting the time elapsed from order placement to delivery.

fields.name: Production volumes
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the quantity of goods or units produced by a company or production facility within a specific reporting period, providing a numerical measure of output for supply chain performance tracking.

fields.name: Manufacturing lead time
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the number of days required to complete the manufacturing process for a product, indicating delivery speed and production turnaround time in a supply chain context.

fields.name: Manufacturing costs
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the monetary cost required to produce individual units or batches in a manufacturing process, capturing variations in production expenses within the supply chain.

fields.name: Inspection results
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field records the outcome status of quality inspections for supply chain items, indicating whether each item has passed, failed, or is awaiting inspection.

fields.name: Defect rates
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the proportion of defective items (as a rate or percentage) found in supply chain batches or shipments, quantifying product quality issues at different stages or locations in the logistics process.

fields.name: Transportation modes
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field indicates the method by which goods are transported within the supply chain, specifying the primary mode—Air, Rail, Road, or Sea—used for shipping.

fields.name: Routes
fields.sample_values: Route A, Route B, Route C
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field identifies distinct transport or delivery pathways (e.g., Route A, Route B, Route C) used for moving goods within the supply chain network.

fields.name: Costs
fields.inferred_semantic_summary: This field represents the monetary expenses incurred for individual supply chain activities or transactions, likely indicating the cost per shipment, order, or unit within supply chain operations.
